# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Linkerd Server and ServerAuthorization resources for microservices. Define which services can communicate with each other. Configure port-based and service account-based authorization rules. This is for our zero-trust service mesh.
# Category: linkerd_authz_missing

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: linkerd-server
---
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  namespace: default
roleRef:
  apiGroups: [""] # "" indicates the core API group (k8s) which is used to create any object. In this case, we're not creating a Kubernetes resource here but directly defining permissions for resources with CoreV1APIResource in v1beta2/v1alpha please check below code
  kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
 name : linkerd-server--default_linkerd-controller --permissionviewaccesssystemnamespace,podsecuritypolicy.kubernetes.io # Permissions for viewAccessSystemNamespace and permissionViewPodSecurityPolicyK8SIO (kind of a Kiali concept)  - this assumes permissions already exist in namespace
rules:
   - apiGroups: [""]     --> Core V1 API group to create any object, e.g., ConfigMap or Pods etc...    like v1beta2/v1alpha onwards for specific kind of objects such as Deployment / Service..etc  depending upon the resources you want permission
   - resourceNames: [linkerd-controller]     --> If we are using any custom namespaces then replace with namespace, otherwise leave empty. The controller name in that case ie., linkerd (default) would be used here as well if not specified else define accordingly..      such cases like v1beta2/v1alpha onwards for specific kind of objects
     resources: 
       - '*'   --> wildcard resource to create all other K8s based resouces, you can change according your needs. (i e., "pods", if we want access permission only pod level)...like v1beta2/v1alpha onwards for specific kind of resources
